work_unit: PD-L003: tighten extractIdFromLine regex in poll-pre-check.js
situation: Derived fix from audit-2026-07-03-013 finding PD-L003 (priority 11, route-fix). extractIdFromLine used \d{4,} which matches timestamps (2026-07-04), version numbers (1.2.3456), and other incidental numerics → wrong stage/job/task IDs silently propagated through drill-down chain.
action: Tightened regex with 3-tier priority: (1) explicit ID labels (id=, taskId:, stage_id=), (2) word-boundary ≥6-digit sequences avoiding dates/versions, (3) original \d{4,} fallback only if no better match — with audit-log entry (precheck-poll action, result=partial, details.reason=regex-fallback) for observability. All 3 call sites updated to pass context label.
outcome_evidence: commit 597c77f in auto/opt-prod-deploy-1782970212; node --check passed; pushed to origin. Regex now prefers labeled IDs and ≥6-digit bounded matches; fallback logs via writeAuditLog for regression detection.
next_suggestion: PD-L003 was last pending fix for audit-2026-07-03-013 cycle. After this merge → cycle-complete → trigger new full audit on next unreviewed project (candidates: sunset-prediction src-core re-audit, or shizi full-stack first deep audit).

audit_findings.finding: extractIdFromLine \d{4,} regex matches timestamps/version numbers causing silent wrong-ID propagation
audit_findings.remediation: 3-tier regex: labeled ID > bounded ≥6-digit > logged fallback
